Trigger: Order looks clean enough to schedule but installability is actually out of scope.
Variable That Changes The Right Answer: Whether the conflict is a minor data cleanup or a true serviceability problem.
Correct Decision: Hold or correct scheduling until serviceability is confirmed; escalate if install scope is genuinely out of scope.
Customer Communication Needed: Explain that the order needs verification before committing to a field-ready appointment.
Crew / Operator Action: Correct attributes; verify scope; escalate to operations management if ambiguity remains.
Downstream Impact: Avoids bad appointment; failed install; and wasted route capacity.
Manifest / System Note: Update OMS/order record with corrected service attributes and serviceability notes.
Resolution Outcome A: Issue is corrected before scheduling and the job proceeds.
Resolution Outcome B: Job is re-scoped; rescheduled; or rejected as sold incorrectly.

Stage: Stage 2: Pre-Delivery Qualification & Site Readiness Check
Trigger: Photos/specs/readiness details show the site is not actually ready.
Variable That Changes The Right Answer: Whether the gap can be fixed remotely before dispatch or makes same-day install impossible.
Correct Decision: Collect the missing info or prep if fixable; otherwise reschedule or escalate before the truck leaves.
Customer Communication Needed: Tell the customer exactly what is missing and whether timing must change.
Crew / Operator Action: Review photos; flag readiness issue; document reason code; update route-feasibility signal.
Downstream Impact: Prevents failed install; route waste; and surprise no-go at the door.
Manifest / System Note: Add photos; notes; readiness status; and reason codes to the record.
Resolution Outcome A: Customer fixes the gap remotely and the job stays on route.
Resolution Outcome B: Job is rescheduled or routed into a different sub-journey.

Trigger: Load verification finds a shortage; missing kit; or manifest mismatch.
Variable That Changes The Right Answer: Whether a same-day substitute or resequencing option exists.
Correct Decision: Replan only if the route can still be completed realistically; do not force a stop without the required product/parts/tools.
Customer Communication Needed: If timing changes materially; inform the customer with a revised window or expectation.
Crew / Operator Action: Update manifest; resequence route if viable; escalate major conflict to ops.
Downstream Impact: Prevents failed stop; late route; and field improvisation with the wrong equipment.
Manifest / System Note: Update route assignment; load verification notes; shortage record; and resequencing notes.
Resolution Outcome A: Same-day substitute or resequencing keeps service viable.
Resolution Outcome B: Stop is removed/rebooked and the route is rebuilt.

Trigger: Crew arrives and finds access/site conditions do not match readiness information.
Variable That Changes The Right Answer: Whether the mismatch is still safe/manageable or a true no-go.
Correct Decision: Proceed only if the revised condition is safe and within authority; otherwise stop and escalate.
Customer Communication Needed: Tell the customer what mismatch was found and whether the job can proceed.
Crew / Operator Action: Document actual conditions with photos; make go/no-go call; escalate if site conflicts with sold service.
Downstream Impact: Prevents injury; damage; unauthorized work; and route-breaking dwell time.
Manifest / System Note: Add field notes; photos; and stop status explaining the mismatch.
Resolution Outcome A: Crew adjusts safely and completes the job.
Resolution Outcome B: Crew records a no-go / reschedule / escalation path.

Trigger: Crew discovers haul-away scope dispute or unsafe disconnection/removal conditions.
Variable That Changes The Right Answer: Whether the condition is safe and within crew authority; whether haul-away was clearly sold/documented.
Correct Decision: Do not force removal when safety or scope is unclear; verify documentation and escalate if needed.
Customer Communication Needed: Explain the scope or safety blocker and what must happen next.
Crew / Operator Action: Check order record; document dispute/condition; escalate damage/safety/scope issues.
Downstream Impact: Prevents damage; leak; unsafe condition; and avoidable complaint escalation.
Manifest / System Note: Add scope dispute notes; photos; and reason codes to the stop record.
Resolution Outcome A: Scope is clarified or removal is completed safely.
Resolution Outcome B: Removal is deferred and routed into a dispute or safety sub-journey.

Trigger: A code; safety; product; or utility issue appears during hookup.
Variable That Changes The Right Answer: Whether the issue is remediable within field authority or requires technical/management escalation.
Correct Decision: Stop standard install work and escalate when the issue crosses safety/code/product boundaries.
Customer Communication Needed: Tell the customer what cannot be completed to code or safely and what happens next.
Crew / Operator Action: Capture evidence; do not improvise outside scope; escalate to technical support or management.
Downstream Impact: Prevents unsafe install; liability; callback; and customer harm.
Manifest / System Note: Add install limitation; defect/code notes; and photos to the field record.
Resolution Outcome A: Issue is resolved within authority and install proceeds to testing.
Resolution Outcome B: Job becomes a revisit; no-go; or technical escalation workflow.

Trigger: Unit fails test or the customer disputes quality/damage/completeness at sign-off.
Variable That Changes The Right Answer: Whether the problem is fixable immediately and whether evidence is clear.
Correct Decision: Resolve within the stop only if it can be done safely and clearly; otherwise capture proof and escalate before departure.
Customer Communication Needed: Explain what was tested; what failed or is disputed; and who owns the next step.
Crew / Operator Action: Run checklist; capture photos/signature or refusal; document dispute; escalate unresolved issue.
Downstream Impact: Prevents undocumented completion; weak dispute posture; and poor handoff to support.
Manifest / System Note: Update POD; test result; dispute notes; and photo record.
Resolution Outcome A: Issue is resolved and clean sign-off is captured.
Resolution Outcome B: Issue is handed off into post-install resolution with strong evidence.

Trigger: A complaint or defect is reported after the visit and ownership is unclear.
Variable That Changes The Right Answer: Severity and cost; plus whether the issue is communication-only; field remedy; or management exception.
Correct Decision: Classify severity correctly and route to the right owner on the first handoff.
Customer Communication Needed: Tell the customer who owns the issue and the expected remedy timeline.
Crew / Operator Action: Review evidence; assign owner; route to customer care; field service; or management based on severity/cost.
Downstream Impact: Prevents dropped cases; duplicated work; long open issues; and customer frustration.
Manifest / System Note: Update case file with evidence; owner; promised timeline; and routing notes.
Resolution Outcome A: Issue is resolved quickly by the correct first owner.
Resolution Outcome B: Issue becomes a higher-severity recovery or claim workflow.
